symptoms.symptom_name: Scattered flat maculopapular spots on the torso

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Flat scattered macules are the typical morphology of a benign viral exanthem.
Any new childhood rash requires the meningococcal (non-blanching) possibility to be actively excluded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lesions blanch completely under pressure (glass/tumbler test), no petechiae or purpura

investigation_details.expected_result: All lesions fade completely under pressure — no non-blanching petechiae or purpura
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without confirming blanching, a non-blanching meningococcal rash cannot be excluded — in telehealth this test must be taught to and performed by the caregiver.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A fully blanching rash argues strongly against meningococcal purpura; a non-blanching rash would be the emergency.
Blanching lesions are consistent with benign dermal vasodilation.
Invasive meningococcal disease is typically febrile and toxic; an afebrile well child is reassuring (though fever can be intermittent, so the safety-net is retained).

symptoms.symptom_name: Alert, active, playful, interactive and feeding normally

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A child who is running around, playing and feeding is at low risk of invasive sepsis (green traffic-light behaviour).

symptoms.symptom_name: No neck stiffness, photophobia, vomiting or drowsiness (no meningism)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of meningism lowers the probability of meningitis, though early disease can lack these signs.
Complete MMR vaccination makes measles far less likely.
Varicella is characterised by vesicles; flat macules argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vitals normal (BP 100/65, HR 95, RR 22, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal perfusion, heart rate and saturations are reassuring — but these are sealed clinician measurements; the AI Doctor cannot obtain them over chat and must reason from the caregiver-observable picture instead.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Well child with a blanching rash
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Blanching rash in an afebrile, active, non-meningitic child is the reassuring constellation that permits home management with safety-netting.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Caregiver-performed glass/tumbler blanching test (with AI-guided instruction)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The single most decisive bedside discriminator between benign exanthem and meningococcal purpura; in telehealth it is taught to and performed by the caregiver, not by the AI.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood tests / lumbar puncture / blood cultures for meningococcal work-up
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not indicated for a well child with a blanching rash; would only follow an in-person emergency assessment if the child were unwell or the rash non-blanching. Over-investigating here reflects anxiety-driven over-triage.
